lloomr 0.1.0.9000
  • Get started
  • Reference

    • lloomr 0.1.0
    • Changelog

Run many structured LLM queries concurrently

Source: R/llm.R
ll_query.Rd

The workhorse behind every LLooM operator (replaces upstream multi_query_gpt_wrapper()). Sends all prompts concurrently through ellmer::parallel_chat_structured() with the given output schema and returns one parsed result per prompt. Failed queries yield NULL (with a warning) rather than aborting the batch, mirroring upstream behavior.

Usage

ll_query(chat, prompts, type, max_active = 10, rpm = 500)

Arguments

chat

An ellmer chat object, e.g. ellmer::chat_openai().

prompts

Character vector or list of prompt strings.

type

An ellmer type spec describing the response shape (see lloom_type()).

max_active

Maximum number of simultaneous requests. Default 10.

rpm

Requests-per-minute throttle. Default 500.

Value

A list with one element per prompt: the parsed result (a list matching type), or NULL if that query failed. Token/cost usage for the call is attached as attribute "usage" (a data frame, or NULL when unavailable).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
chat <- ellmer::chat_openai(model = "gpt-5.4-nano", echo = "none")
prompts <- sapply(c("The economy is improving.", "Media trust is falling."),
  function(ex) render_prompt(
    lloom_prompt("distill_summarize"),
    list(ex = ex, seeding_phrase = "", n_bullets = 2, n_words = "3-5")
  ))
res <- ll_query(chat, prompts, lloom_type("distill_summarize"))
str(res)
attr(res, "usage")  # tokens and dollars
} # }
